1	package server
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"time"
7	
8		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
9		ddxconfig "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
10	)
11	
12	// ReviewDispatcher dispatches review-session turns to the reviewer harness
13	// and records each resulting turn in the session's turns.jsonl.
14	type ReviewDispatcher struct {
15		ProjectRoot string
16		Store       *ReviewSessionStore
17		// Runner, when non-nil, replaces the production dispatch path. Inject in
18		// tests to return canned results without spinning up a real agent service.
19		Runner agent.AgentRunner
20	}
21	
22	// DispatchReviewTurn assembles a prompt from the session state and the
23	// incoming user turn, invokes the reviewer harness in no-tool mode, records
24	// the resulting reviewer turn in turns.jsonl, and returns that turn.
25	//
26	// No-tool enforcement:
27	//   - claude harness: agent.PermissionsReadOnlyReviewer ("readonly") is set on
28	//     every dispatch request. The claude CLI permission system enforces this by
29	//     disabling tool use before the request reaches the model. The dispatch
30	//     boundary (agent.ValidateReadOnlyReviewerDispatch) gates the call and
31	//     returns agent.ReviewReadOnlyEnforcementError for incapable harnesses.
32	//   - codex harness: best-effort. Codex is not in agent.readOnlyCapableHarnesses;
33	//     when the resolved harness is codex, ValidateReadOnlyReviewerDispatch
34	//     returns ReviewReadOnlyEnforcementError and the dispatch is rejected.
35	//     Callers that require codex must switch to a harness that supports
36	//     read-only enforcement (e.g., claude or agent).
37	func (d *ReviewDispatcher) DispatchReviewTurn(ctx context.Context, session ReviewSession, userTurn ReviewTurn) (ReviewTurn, error) {
38		// Include the incoming user turn before rendering so the reviewer sees
39		// the full turn history including the latest message.
40		sessionWithTurn := session
41		sessionWithTurn.Turns = append(append([]ReviewTurn(nil), session.Turns...), userTurn)
42	
43		rendered, err := RenderReviewPrompt(ReviewPromptRenderInput{
44			Session: sessionWithTurn,
45		})
46		if err != nil {
47			return ReviewTurn{}, fmt.Errorf("review dispatcher: assemble prompt: %w", err)
48		}
49	
50		var result *agent.Result
51		if d.Runner != nil {
52			// Test injection path: pass permissions directly in RunArgs so tests
53			// can verify that PermissionsReadOnlyReviewer was set.
54			result, err = d.Runner.Run(agent.RunArgs{
55				Context:     ctx,
56				Prompt:      rendered.Prompt,
57				WorkDir:     d.ProjectRoot,
58				Permissions: agent.PermissionsReadOnlyReviewer,
59				Role:        "reviewer",
60			})
61		} else {
62			rcfg, _ := ddxconfig.LoadAndResolve(d.ProjectRoot, ddxconfig.CLIOverrides{})
63			runtime := agent.AgentRunRuntime{
64				Prompt:              rendered.Prompt,
65				WorkDir:             d.ProjectRoot,
66				PermissionsOverride: agent.PermissionsReadOnlyReviewer,
67				ClearRoutingPins:    true,
68				ClearProfile:        true,
69				ClearMaxPower:       true,
70				Role:                "reviewer",
71			}
72			result, err = agent.RunWithConfigViaService(ctx, d.ProjectRoot, rcfg, runtime)
73		}
74		if err != nil {
75			return ReviewTurn{}, fmt.Errorf("review dispatcher: dispatch: %w", err)
76		}
77	
78		output := ""
79		costUSD := 0.0
80		if result != nil {
81			output = result.Output
82			costUSD = result.CostUSD
83		}
84	
85		reviewerTurn := ReviewTurn{
86			Actor:     "reviewer",
87			Content:   output,
88			CostUSD:   costUSD,
89			CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
90		}
91		if d.Store != nil {
92			if appendErr := d.Store.AppendTurn(session.ID, reviewerTurn); appendErr != nil {
93				return ReviewTurn{}, fmt.Errorf("review dispatcher: record turn: %w", appendErr)
94			}
95		}
96	
97		return reviewerTurn, nil
98	}
99

data.text:
Now I understand the situation. The ReviewSessionStore and ReviewDispatcher are a complete, tested implementation that's currently dead code. The production code is using an in-memory service instead. I'll wire the production implementation by creating a ReviewSessionService wrapper around these components.

Let me create a decisions log first, then implement the wiring:

50	
51	// serverPromptCapBytes caps the size of inline prompt bodies accepted on
52	// server-side ingress (POST /api/agent/run). FEAT-022 §9 / Stage D2. It is
53	// a package-level variable rather than a literal so tests can lower the
54	// cap without writing multi-MB request bodies. Production callers must not
55	// mutate it.
56	var serverPromptCapBytes = evidence.DefaultMaxPromptBytes
57	
58	// serverBuildSHA returns the build's VCS revision for inclusion on
59	// operator-prompt audit events. Falls back to "unknown" when build info is
60	// unavailable (e.g. `go test` without -buildvcs or development builds).
61	func serverBuildSHA() string {
62		info, ok := debug.ReadBuildInfo()
63		if !ok {
64			return "unknown"
65		}
66		for _, s := range info.Settings {
67			if s.Key == "vcs.revision" && s.Value != "" {
68				return s.Value
69			}
70		}
71		return "unknown"
72	}
73	
74	// serverInlineCapBytes caps inline content bodies returned on egress paths
75	// — MCP tool responses and REST JSON responses that carry library-doc,
76	// execution-bundle, diff, session, or persona text. FEAT-022 §10 / §11.
77	// Stage E1. Package-level variable so tests can lower the cap; production
78	// callers must not mutate it.
79	var serverInlineCapBytes = evidence.DefaultMaxInlinedFileBytes
80	
81	// mcpText constructs a text-typed mcpContent whose Text body is bounded by
82	// serverInlineCapBytes via evidence.ClampOutput. The Truncated and
83	// OriginalBytes fields are always populated (false/len(text) when the body
84	// fits within the cap). This is the single mcpContent{Type:"text"} literal
85	// site in this package — every other caller must route through this helper
86	// so static analysis (TestEvidencePrimitiveUsage) can guarantee no
87	// unbounded text egress on the MCP surface. FEAT-022 §10 / Stage E1.
88	func mcpText(text string) mcpContent {
89		clamped, truncated, originalBytes := evidence.ClampOutput(text, serverInlineCapBytes)
90		// evidence:allow-unbounded reason="single mcpContent{Type:\"text\"} literal site for the package; Text is sourced from evidence.ClampOutput above (FEAT-022 §10 Stage E1)"
91		return mcpContent{Type: "text", Text: clamped, Truncated: truncated, OriginalBytes: originalBytes}
92	}
93	
94	// beadHubCloser is the minimal interface the Server requires from its bead
95	// lifecycle hub. *bead.WatcherHub satisfies this interface.
96	type beadHubCloser interface {
97		SubscribeLifecycle(projectID string) (<-chan bead.LifecycleEvent, func())
98		Close()
99	}
100	
101	// Server is the DDx HTTP server exposing REST and MCP endpoints.
102	type Server struct {
103		Addr        string
104		WorkingDir  string
105		TsnetConfig *config.TsnetConfig
106		mux         *http.ServeMux
107		startTime   time.Time
108		workers     *WorkerManager
109		beadHub     beadHubCloser
110		state       *ServerState
111	
112		// csrfTokens issues and validates CSRF tokens for write mutations on
113		// the GraphQL endpoint (Story 15 / operator-prompts). The token is
114		// generated once at server construction and exposed to clients via
115		// GET /api/csrf-token (localhost-gated).
116		csrfTokens *ddxgraphql.StaticCSRFTokenStore
117		// operatorPromptIdempotency deduplicates operatorPromptSubmit calls by
118		// idempotency key within a 24-hour window. Process-local; survives
119		// only for the lifetime of the server process.
120		operatorPromptIdempotency *ddxgraphql.MemoryIdempotencyCache
121		// operatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist is the per-project allowlist of
122		// localhost identity actors permitted to auto-approve their own
123		// operator-prompt submissions and to invoke operatorPromptApprove. The
124		// locked Story 15 decision restricts approval to configured-localhost
125		// identities; ts-net peers are never eligible. Seeded from
126		// DDX_OPERATOR_PROMPT_ALLOWLIST (comma-separated) at server construction
127		// and may be overridden by tests via SetOperatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist.
128		operatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist []string
129	
130		routePatterns []string // every pattern registered via route(); used by gate tests
131	
132		// HubMode is true once EnableHubMode has been called; the federation
133		// HTTP routes are mounted only in that case (B14.3 AC: "Routes mounted
134		// only when --hub-mode").
135		HubMode bool
136		hub     *federationHub
137	
138		// SpokeMode is true once EnableSpokeMode has been called. Used together
139		// with HubMode to compute node.federation_role:
140		//   neither   → "standalone"
141		//   hub only  → "hub"
142		//   spoke only→ "spoke"
143		//   both      → "hub_spoke"
144		SpokeMode bool
145		spoke     *federationSpoke
146	
147		// gqlHandler is the singleton gqlgen HTTP handler. LAYER 2 of the
148		// GraphQL multi-project fix (ddx-055e8d32) builds this once at first
149		// request and reuses it across both /graphql and the scoped
150		// /api/projects/{project}/graphql route — per-request reconstruction
